observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the LCE require the MA to

(a) strictly review/analyze prior year’s transactions to locate the unidentified discrepancies and take up the necessary adjustments to correct the balances of the affected reciprocal accounts; and


(b) conduct regular review and reconciliation of reciprocal accounts to correct and prevent discrepancies in the future.
observations.reason: This is due to non -availability of records or data with regards to the Prior Years, that can be used as basis for the necessary adjustments.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the LCE require the

(a) Municipal Human Resources and Management Office (MHRMO) to submit a copy of the Minutes of Meeting directing the DHs to do the scheduling of employees who reported to duty during the period of ECQ;

(b) DHs to submit the schedule of employees required to work during the pandemic stating the dates and purposes as directed by the LCE together with the logbook of attendance and daily time records and

(c) MAO to closely verify the correctness of the payrolls/disbursements, and make the necessary adjustments when needed, prior to payment.

We reiterated our recommendation that the LCE require the MA and MT to

(a) make representations with the GSIS, HDMF and PhilHealth, for reconciliation of records to address promptly the under/over remittances of premiums and loans; and

(b) ensure that the premiums and loan amortization withheld from the salaries of Municipal employees are remitted intact to GSIS, HDMF and PHIC to correct the previous years’ deficiencies.
observations.reason:
Presently unable to seek reconciliation with the concerned agencies due to the Covid-19 pandemic.

Partially Implemented. Unable to reconcile with the concerned agencies due to the pandemic.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated that the LCE direct the MA to require the persons liable to immediately comply with Section 7.1 of RRSA on the settlement of the disallowances.
observations.reason: Disallowances are under appeal with the COA Central Office.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) instruct (a) the Municipal Assessor to prepare and submit the Assessment Roll to the MTon or before the 31st day of December of each year; (b) the MT to (i) use the said roll in preparing an updated CLT bearing the taxpayer’s names, addresses and amounts due and collectible during the year; and (ii) send out notices of tax delinquencies and employ other collection strategies that would increase the collection of RPT/SET income; and (c) the MA to set-up the RPT/SET Receivables each year based on the CLT prepared by the MT.

We recommended that the LCE (a) require the GSO and MAO to (i) strictly comply with the required maintenance of PPELCs and PCs, respectively, for each class of PPE to completely record and update the acquisition, adjustment and disposal thereof; (ii) exert efforts to complete the physical inventory of all PPE of the Municipality and reconcile the report with the MAO records; and (iii)  submit the physical inventory reports on PPE to the AT  for review and validation not later than January 31 of each year pursuant to existing rules and regulations; (b) create a Special Committee to have the parcels of land covered by Transfer Certificates of Title issued under the name of the Municipality; and (c) require the MA to  recognize in the book of accounts  the  33  parcels of land  with  assessed value of ₱26,741,861.07.

We recommended that the LCE  require the: (a) MA torecognize  in the TF books  the unexpended balances of the LDRRMF QRF and MOOE for CY 2021 totaling ₱3,721,026.55 under the account “Trust Liability-DRRMF”  at the end of the year and  to treat as continuing  the unexpended balance of CO in the GF books; (b)  MT to transfer the corresponding cash back up in the TF depository bank; and (c)  strictly follow the relevant rules and regulations affecting the recording of the transfer and utilization of LDRRMF to reflect the correct balances of the related accounts.

We recommended that the LCE  require (a) the MAO and the MTO to return the unutilized PDAF to the National Treasury totaling ₱5,347,005.92 and the unexpended balance from the BUB-Integrated Community Concreting of Road Project to the source agency amounting to ₱50,256.16; and (b) the MAO to (i) reclassify the funds erroneously recognized as trust liability instead of income; and (ii) proceed with the conduct of writing off the dormant and unsubstantiated accounts amounting to ₱621,565.37  in order to clear both the GF and TF books pursuant to the procedures prescribed by COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.

We recommended that the LCE require the MA to (a) strictly review/analyze the prior years’ transactions to locate the unidentified discrepancies and take up the necessary adjustments to correct the balances of the affected reciprocal accounts; and (b) conduct regular review and reconciliation of reciprocal accounts to correct and prevent discrepancies in the future.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) instruct the 

(a) Municipal Treasurer to 

prepare an updated CLT bearing the names, amounts due and collectible during the year, and submit the same to the MA as the basis in setting up of RPT and SET Receivables;

ensure that the list of taxpayers bear vital information, such as the taxpayer’s addresses, to facilitate the sending of notices of tax delinquencies and other follow up communications relative to tax campaign and collection; and 

implement collection strategies that would increase the collection of RPT/SET income; 

(b) Municipal Assessor to prepare and submit the Assessment Roll  to the Municipal Treasurer as basis of the latter to prepare the CLT; and 

(c)  MA to use the CLT prepared by the MT as basis in recording the RPT/SET Receivables at the beginning of the year.
observations.reason: Most of the accounts were delinquent for a very long time and the probability of collection was very low.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the LCE

require the GSO and MAO to

(i) strictly comply with the required maintenance of PPELCs and PCs, respectively, for each class of PPE to completely record and update the acquisition, adjustments and disposal thereof;

(ii) exert efforts to complete the physical inventory of all PPE of the Municipality and reconcile the report with the MAO records; and


(iii)  submit the physical inventory reports on PPE to the Audit Team for review and validation not later than January 31 of each year pursuant to existing rules and regulations; and

(b) create a Special Committee to have the parcels of land covered by Transfer Certificates of Titles issued under the name of the Municipality, if warranted, for proper recognition in the books.
observations.managementAction: Inventory Report for CY2021 was already submitted.

observations.recommendation: We commended the LCE for her substantial compliance with the requirements on the allocation, utilization, accounting, and reporting guidelines on the use of the LDRRMF. However, for the noted documentary requirements on procurements totaling ₱8,929,194.66, we recommended that the LCE instruct the members of the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to submit the lacking eligibility requirements of the concerned Contractors.

We commended the LCE and the authorized LGU representative, the DSWD Officer and other concerned officials for imposing proper measures in profiling the beneficiaries specifically in times of calamities such as the

(a) conduct of strict house to house screening in accordance with the requirements of DSWD MC No. 9 series of 2020;

(b) proper conduct of distribution of SAP to the qualified beneficiaries; and 

(c) complying with the required documentation in the granting and liquidation of the cash advance.

We commended the LCE and the concerned local officials in their compliance with the appropriation and utilization of funds for PPAs under the BGCM prescribed policy guidelines. However, for the noted documentary requirements, we recommended that the LCE instruct the members of the Bids and awards Committee (BAC) to require the submission of the eligibility documents of suppliers.
observations.managementAction: Implemented

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the LCE require the GAD council to

(a) appropriate at least five percent (5%) of their total budgets for GAD plans and programs;

(b) establish a GAD database; and


(c) implement appropriate mechanism in planning and programming in accordance with the provisions of PCW-DILG-NEDA-DBM-JMC2013-01 with amendments under JMC No. 2016-01 to ensure that the utilization of the GAD Fund will truly address the gender issues in the locality.
observations.managementAction: The GPB and GAD AR were already submitted.
observations.reason:
Not Implemented.
The GAD database was still not established.

Partially Implemented

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendation that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer   to

(a) immediately remit the amounts due to the BIR and hold the erring officials liable for the penal charges to be imposed by the BIR for late payment; and

(b) strictly comply with the rules and regulations on the remittance of withheld taxes as prescribed under RMO No. 23-2014 and RR No. 2-98.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive required the

BAC and its Secretariat, GSO, EO and the Accounting Office to comply with the provisions of the RA 9184 regarding the alternative mode of procurements; 

(b)  Municipal Accountant to check the completeness of supporting documents before effecting the payments of any obligations in view of the contracts’ deliverables and 

(c) BAC and its Secretariat, GSO, EO and the Accounting Office to submit the lacking documents above-mentioned.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) instruct the (a) Municipal Treasurer to (i) prepare an updated CLT bearing the names, amounts due and collectible during the year, and submit the same to the MA as the basis in setting up of RPT and SET Receivables; (ii) ensure that the list of taxpayers bear vital information, such as the taxpayer’s addresses, to facilitate the sending of notices of tax delinquencies and other follow up communications relative to tax campaign and collection; and (iii) implement collection strategies that would increase the collection of RPT/SET income; (b) Municipal Assessor to prepare and submit the Assessment Roll  to the Municipal Treasurer as basis of the latter to prepare the CLT; and (c)  MA to use the CLT  prepared by the MT as basis in recording the RPT/SET Receivables at the beginning of the year.

We recommended that the LCE (a) require the GSO and MAO to (i) strictly comply with the required maintenance of PPELCs and PCs, respectively, for each class of PPE to completely record and update the acquisition, adjustments and disposal thereof; (ii) exert efforts to complete the physical inventory of all PPE of the Municipality and reconcile the report with the MAO records; and (iii)  submit  the physical inventory reports on PPE to the Audit Team for review and validation not later than January 31 of each year pursuant to existing rules and regulations; and (b) create a Special Committee to have the parcels of land covered by Transfer Certificates of Titles  issued under the name of the  Municipality, if warranted, for proper recognition in the books.

We recommended that the LCE require the MA to (a) strictly review/analyze prior year’s transactions to locate the unidentified discrepancies and take up the necessary adjustments to correct the balances of the affected reciprocal accounts; and (b) conduct regular review and reconciliation of reciprocal accounts to correct and prevent discrepancies in the future.

We commended the LCE for her substantial compliance with the requirements on the allocation, utilization, accounting, and reporting guidelines on the use of the LDRRMF. However, for the noted documentary requirements on procurements totaling ₱8,929,194.66, we recommended that the LCE instruct the members of the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to submit the lacking eligibility requirements of the concerned Contractors.

We commended the LCE and the concerned local officials in their compliance with the appropriation and utilization of funds for PPAs under the BGCM prescribed policy guidelines. However, for the noted documentary requirements, we recommended that the LCE instruct the members of the Bids and awards Committee (BAC) to require the submission of the eligibility documents of suppliers.

We commended the LCE for managing to implement the PPAs included in the AIP and pursuant to DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-01.  However, we recommended that the LCE (a)  require the GSO Chief and  Municipal Engineer (ME)  to  hire laborers/workers who are  residents of  the Municipality  for projects implemented by Administration; (b)  undertake procurement of infrastructure projects through competitive  public bidding and resort to the alternative methods of procurement only in  highly exceptional cases; (c) require the BAC members to  submit the technical eligibility documents and other contract supporting documents; and (d) require the MA to check the completeness of supporting documents before payments are made.

We recommended that the LCE require the (a) Municipal Human Resources and Management Office (MHRMO)  to submit a copy of the Minutes of Meeting directing the DHs to do the scheduling of employees who  reported  to duty during the period of ECQ; (b) DHs to submit the schedule of employees required to work during the pandemic stating the dates and purposes as directed by the LCE together with the logbook of attendance and daily time records  and (c) MAO to closely verify the correctness of the payrolls/disbursements, and make the necessary adjustments when needed, prior to payment.

We recommended that the LCE require the GAD council to (a) appropriate at least five percent (5%) of their total budgets for GAD plans and programs; (b) establish a GAD database; and (c) implement appropriate mechanism in planning and programming in accordance with the provisions of PCW-DILG-NEDA-DBM JMC 2013-01 with amendments under JMC No. 2016-01 to ensure that the utilization of the GAD Fund will truly address the gender issues in the locality.

We reiterated our recommendation that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer   to (a) immediately remit the amounts due to the BIR and hold the erring officials liable for the penal charges to be imposed by the BIR for late payment; and (b) strictly comply with the rules and regulations on the remittance of withheld taxes as prescribed under RMO No. 23-2014 and RR No. 2-98.

We reiterated our recommendation that the LCE require the MA and MT  to (a) make representations with the GSIS, HDMF and PhilHealth, for reconciliation of records to address promptly the under/over remittances of premiums and loans; and (b) ensure that the premiums and loan amortization withheld from the salaries of Municipal employees are remitted intact to GSIS,  HDMF and PHIC to correct the previous years’ deficiencies.
We reiterated that the LCE direct the MA to require the persons liable to immediately comply with Section 7.1 of RRSA on the settlement of the disallowances.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the:

Municipal Treasurer to

prepare an updated Certified List of Taxpayers, bearing the names, amount due and collectible during the year, and submit the same to the accounting unit as the basis in setting up of RPT/SET receivable in the succeeding year; 

 ensure that the list of taxpayers bear vital information, such as the addresses, to facilitate, if necessary, the sending of notices and other follow up communications relative to tax campaign activities and other collection strategy that may be  implemented by Management; and 

(iii) introduce collection strategy that would significantly help increase the tax collection of the Municipality.

Municipal Accountant to

observe the provisions of Section 20 of the New Government Accounting (NGAS) Manual; and

(ii) use the Certified List of Taxpayers and tax due prepared by the Office of the Municipal Treasurer as the basis in recording the RPT/SET receivables at the beginning of the year.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive

require the GSO and MAO to

(i) strictly comply with the required maintenance of complete and updated PPELC and PC, respectively for each class of property and to record the acquisition, adjustments and disposal thereof; and  

(ii) exert extra effort to complete the physical inventory of all property, plant, and equipment of the Municipal Government and reconcile the report with the accounting records; and 

(iii) observe the consistent submission of the physical inventory reports on PPEs to the Office of the Auditor for review and validation not later than January 31 of each year pursuant to existing rules and regulations; and 

(b) create a special committee to account for its real properties specially the lots covered only by tax declarations for titling and proper recording in the books.

We recommended that LCE require the Municipal  Accountant to 

(a) review/analyze prior year’s transactions to unidentified discrepancies and take up the necessary adjustments to correct the balances of the affected accounts; and 

(b) conduct regular review and reconciliation of reciprocal accounts and effect adjustments of reconciling items periodically, as identified and substantiated, to prevent existence of long outstanding unadjusted reconciling items.

We recommended that Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accounting Office to

(a) comply with the prescribed accounting entries in the transfer and utilization of the LDRRMF in accordance with PPSAS;

(b) record the unexpended balances of the CY2019 to the TL- LDRRMF totaling ₱2,553,161.63; and

(c) henceforth, strictly follow the relevant rules and regulations affecting the recording of the transfer and utilization of LDRRMF to reflect the correct balances of the related financial accounts.

We reiterated our recommendation that Local Chief Executive require the Accounting Office to

(a) make representations with the GSIS, Philhealth, and Pag-IBIG for reconciliation of records to address promptly the under/(over) remittances of premiums and loans; and

(b) henceforth, ensure that the premiums and loan amortization withheld from salaries of Municipal employees are remitted to GSIS, PHIC and HDMF intact in order not to aggravate further the previous years’ deficiencies.

We recommended Local Chief Executive

(a) require the General Services Officer (GSO) to consistently observe submission of approved contracts/ purchase orders together with its supporting documents within five working days upon approval as required under COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009 to facilitate timely review and evaluation; and

(b) direct compliance with COA Circular 95-006 dated  May 18, 1995 in the submission of  Inspection and Acceptance Reports (IAR) to prevent the recurrence of similar lapses and to facilitate the conduct of  inspection/validation of delivery in the future and to timely communicate to management the deficiencies, if any, for corrective action.

observations.managementAction: AAR CY-2018 | The MAO recognized in its CY 2019 Financial Statements, an increase in Building account amounting to ₱3,194,831.03. This represents the completed Multi-Purpose Building constructed beside the Municipal Hall Building.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accounting Office to 

(a) comply with the prescribed accounting entries in the transfer and utilization of the LDRRMF in accordance with PPSAS; 



(b) record the unexpended balances of the CY2018 to the TL- LDRRMF totaling ₱1,448,102.63; and 





(c) henceforth, strictly follow the relevant rules and regulations affecting the recording of the transfer and utilization of LDRRMF to reflect the correct balances of the related financial accounts.
observations.managementAction: AAR CY-2018 | The MAO reflected the payment of Accounts payable pertaining to CY 2018 LDRRMF-MOOE amounting to ₱539,100.00 in its CY 2019 FS; the balance of CY 2018 LDRRMF-QRF and MOOE amounting to ₱909,002.63 was transferred to TL-LDRRMF.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) as head of the MDC ensure an effective planning and specific identification of the 20% DF through series of meeting with the members of the MDC and stakeholders to  ensure that 

(a) other areas of development are given substantial consideration in planning and budgeting of the 20% DF; 

(b) non-priority projects of the 20% DF are not included in the prioritization of projects; 

(c) programmed projects are fully implemented; and 

(d) projects implemented by the barangays in which there was an augmentation from the Municipal’s 20% DF are included in the priority development projects of the Municipality.

We also recommended that the 

(a) Engineering Office observe the appropriate posting of advertisement on all projects to be implemented; and 

(b) Municipal Accounting Office ensure the completeness of supporting documents prior to payment.

We reiterated our recommendation that Management require the Accounting Office to 

(a) make representations with the GSIS, Philhealth, and Pag-IBIG for reconciliation of records to address promptly the under/(over) remittances of premiums and loans; and

(b) henceforth, ensure that the premiums and loan amortization withheld from salaries of Municipal employees are remitted to GSIS, PHIC and HDMF intact in order not to aggravate further the previous years’ deficiencies.

We reiterated our recommendation that Management require the concerned accountable officials to 

(a) immediately remit the amounts due to BIR and hold the erring officials liable for the penal charges to be imposed by the BIR for late payment; and 

(b) henceforth, strictly comply with the rules and regulations on the remittance of withheld taxes as prescribed under Revenue Memorandum Order No. 23-2014 dated June 20, 2014 and Revenue Regulation No. 2-98 dated April 17, 1998.
We reiterated that Management enforce the settlement of disallowed amount of ₱484,500.00 affirmed by the COA Regional Office No. III.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) require the MAO and MTO to (a) make a representation with the Authorized Government Depository Bank (AGDB) concerned to shed light on the identified bank error of ₱6,000.00 in 2004 for proper recognition in the books of the Municipality; and (b) reconcile their cash records regularly to ensure that all transactions affecting the cash accounts are captured in the MTO cashbooks and provide a reliable Cash in Bank balances per cashbook.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) (a) require the AOs to strictly follow the prescribed liquidation period for cash advances; and (b) prevent granting of multiple cash advances to individual AOs in order to prevent exposing huge funds to possible loss.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) instruct the (a) Municipal Treasurer to (i) prepare an updated Certified List of Taxpayers bearing the names, amount due and collectible during the year, and submit the same to the MAO as the basis in setting up of RPT/SET Receivable in the succeeding years; (ii) ensure that the list of taxpayers bear vital information, such as the addresses, to facilitate, if necessary, the sending of notices and other follow up communications relative to tax campaign activities and other collection strategies that may be implemented by Management; and (iii) introduce collection strategies that would significantly help increase the tax income of the Municipality; and (b) Municipal Accountant to observe the provisions of Section 20 of the NGAS Manual and use of the CLT prepared by the MTO in setting up the RPT/SET Receivables at the beginning of the year.

We recommended that the LCE (a) require the (i) GSO and MAO to strictly comply with the required maintenance of complete and updated PPELC and PC, respectively, for each class of property and to record the acquisition, adjustments and disposal thereof to facilitate reconciliation of records; (ii) created Inventory Committee to exert extra effort to complete the physical inventory of all PPE owned by the Municipality; (iii) GSO to observe the consistent submission of the physical inventory reports on PPEs to the Audit Team for review and validation not later than January 31 of each year pursuant to existing rules and regulations; and (b) create a special committee to account for the parcels of land covered only by tax declarations under the name of the Municipality for titling and proper recording in the books.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the General Services Office to (a) cause the preparation of the updated Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property together with the corresponding assessment, evaluation and appraisal reports; (b) facilitate the submission of request for disposal of said properties to the AT for inspection, evaluation and review by the COA Technical Services Office; (c) ensure that after receipt of favorable COA recommendations on the request,  undertake the immediate disposal of the subject properties to prevent further deterioration, decline of value and in order to generate additional funds from sale thereof; and (d) prepare and submit the regular schedule of disposal of assets once the same became unserviceable and beyond economical repairs to avoid the accumulation of unserviceable properties in the storage facility and to prevent the recurrence of similar lapses.

We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant to (a) review/analyze prior year’s transactions to identify the discrepancies and take up the necessary adjustments to correct the balances of the affected accounts; and (b) conduct regular review and reconciliation of reciprocal accounts and effect adjustments of reconciling items periodically, as identified and substantiated, to prevent existence of long outstanding unadjusted reconciling items.

We recommended that the LCE require the (a) BAC and its Secretariat, GSO, Municipal Engineering Office (MEO) and the MAO to comply with the provisions of the  CY 2016 IRR of  R.A. No. 9184 regarding the alternative modes of procurement; (b) Municipal Accountant to check the completeness of supporting documents before effecting the payments of any obligations in view of the contracts’ deliverables; and (c) BAC and its Secretariat, GSO, MEO and the MAO to submit the noted lacking documents.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the Municipal Accounting Office to (a) record the unexpended balances of the LDRRMF for CY2019 totaling ₱2,543,000.18 to the TL-LDRRMF account; (b) revert back to the General Fund (GF) the unused LDRRMF of CYs 2013 and 2014 totaling ₱6,776,040.68; and (c) strictly follow the relevant rules and regulations affecting the recording of the transfer and utilization of LDRRMF to reflect the correct balances of the related financial statement accounts.

We recommended that the LCE (a) continue to monitor the review and approval timeline of the LSWMP for its prompt implementation and realization; (b) continue the planned improvements for the MRF as well as the trainings lined up for the MRF personnel; (c) intensify its information campaign on proper waste segregation and waste minimization by organizing barangay level hands-on trainings/seminars where the public can have a first-hand experience on how to properly segregate household garbage and to apply waste minimization measures like composting, recycling, etc. in their own backyard; (d) designate marshals who shall monitor the compliance of the public to local ordinances on solid waste management; and (e) strictly adhere to the provision of  R.A. No. 9184 relative to Alternative Modes of Procurement.

We reiterated our recommendation that the LCE require the MAO to (a) make representations with GSIS, PhilHealth, and Pag-IBIG for reconciliation of records to address promptly the under/over remittances of premiums and loans; and (b) henceforth, ensure that the premiums and loan amortization withheld from salaries of Municipal employees are remitted to GSIS, PHIC and HDMF intact in order not to aggravate further the previous years’ deficiencies.

We reiterated our recommendation that the LCE require the concerned accountable officials to (a) immediately remit the amounts due to BIR and hold the erring officials liable for the penal charges to be imposed by the BIR for late payment; and (b) henceforth, strictly comply with the rules and regulations on the remittance of withheld taxes as prescribed under Revenue Memorandum Order No. 23-2014 dated June 20, 2014 and Revenue Regulation No. 2-98 dated April 17, 1998.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the Municipal HRMO to (a) prepare and release policy guidelines to address the issues on the necessity and propriety of hiring job order personnel; (b) hire job order personnel based on necessity and skills/competencies and who should be identified/attributed to specific local projects authorized by the Sanggunian for a period not exceeding six months; (c) avoid the hiring of job orders whose functions overlap/duplicate those of the regular employees of the Municipality, especially the accountable officers; and (d) submit to the Audit Team the CoS and copy of government-issued valid IDs to evaluate the necessity and validity of all JO personnel employed by the Municipality as of December 31, 2019.

We recommended that the LCE (a) require the GSO to consistently observe the submission of approved contracts/purchase orders together with its supporting documents within five working days upon approval as required under COA Circular No. 2009-001 to facilitate timely review and evaluation; and (b) direct compliance with COA Circular 95-006 in the submission of  Inspection and Acceptance Reports (IAR) to prevent the recurrence of similar lapses and to facilitate the conduct of  inspection/validation of delivery in the future and to timely communicate to management the deficiencies, if any, for corrective action.
We reiterated our recommendation that the LCE require the persons liable to cause the settlement of the disallowance in which no appeal was made.

We recommended that Management require 

(a) the MAO to 

(i) effect the recording of the liquidation of the DPWH funds in the books amounting to ₱2,385,794.08; and 



(ii) reclassify the funds erroneously identified as trust liability instead of income; 



(b) the MAO and the Municipal Treasurer’s Office to return the unutilized PDAF to the National Treasury totaling ₱5,347,005.92, and the unexpended balance of trust funds to the source agencies totaling ₱51,302.66; and 

(c) instruct the MAO to  exert efforts in retrieving records pertaining to ₱585,965.37 funds in order to facilitate the return of unused funds to source agencies.
observations.managementAction: AAR CY-2018 | The MAO recorded the liquidation of the DPWH funds amounting to ₱2,385,794.08 in March 2019 in its General Fund Books.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the MAO continue to exert its best possible means to retrieve further the previous records relative to the recording of the loans payables to reflect the true balance of the account.

We recommended that the LSB 

(a) refrain from charging expenses that are beyond the priority projects of the SEF; 



(b) allocate funding for ECCD programs, projects and activities; and 

(c) chairmen call for a frequent meeting with the members of the LSB 

(i) to monitor and evaluate whether the PPAs charged under the SEF largely addressed the true issues in the education system in the public sector; and 

(ii) plan the allocation of the SEF exclusively on the priority projects enumerated under JC No. 01.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive 

(a) require the Municipal Treasurer Office (MTO) and the MAO 

(i) to determine the noted discrepancies between their records to effect the necessary adjustments in their books; and 



(ii) henceforth, to monitor their cash in bank balances through regular reconciliation of their records; and 

(b) require the MTO 

(i) to  make necessary adjustments in the CRAAF prepared by the MAO; and henceforth, 

(ii) to prepare the CRAAF based on the their records as the preparation of such is one of the functions of the Office and not of the MAO; and 

(iii) to monitor the validity of the fidelity bond to prevent the incurrence of granting cash advances to accountable officers with expired bond.
observations.managementAction:
AAR CY-2018 | GF balances of MTO and MAO did not tally. This is due to Non-Reconciliation of records. 








4 out of 5 AOs with expired bonds within August 20 to October 09, 2019 were still granted cash advances.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) require the 

(a) MTO to prepare the RCI with the date based on the actual issuance of check and submit to the MAO together with the paid DVs for preparation of the JEV; and 

(b) MAO to: 
(i) prepare the JEV for the CkDJ based on the RCI submitted by the MTO to reflect the true cash in bank balances in the financial statements; 

(ii) reconcile records with the LBP particularly on the alleged bank error of ₱6,000.00; and 



(iii) henceforth, monitor closely the preparation of the BRS in order to effect the adjustments in the books, if any, in a timely fashion.
observations.managementAction:
Observation No.1
AAR CY-2017 | The MTO now prepares and submits RCI to the MAO.










The MAO reiterated its difficulty to track any document regarding the error as it occurred during the time of the previous Accountant.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the concerned accountable officer to 

(a) issue PCV for every payment made out of the PCF to ensure that only authorized petty expenses are paid out of the PCF; and 

(b) limit the payments from PCF to ₱15,000.00 and below pursuant to existing rules and regulations.
observations.managementAction:
Observation No.3
AAR CY-2017 |

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to 

(a) revert those payables still in the books for years, with no filed administrative or judicial claim, and not covered with perfected contract; and 

(b) work on the abnormal balance in the records of the Other Payables account
observations.managementAction:
Observation No.5
AAR CY-2017 | Management work on checking the accounts and gathering information.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) 

(a) ensure that:

(i) the infrastructure projects included in the LDIP for 2017-2019 that aim to direct the economic and social development of the Municipality be implemented efficiently and effectively by ensuring that the PPAs presented in the AIP were sourced from the LDIP of the Municipality, except on cases that require immediate action, provided further that the implementation of such passed the Local Development Council and resolved by the Sangguniang Bayan; 

(ii) the 20% DF is utilized in accordance with the existing guidelines; 



(iii) the procurements/projects are not split into smaller quantities and amount as provided for in R.A. No. 9184 to evade public bidding; and 

(b) require the:

(i) BAC, Engineering Office, and concerned officials to re-visit and review the process in implementing projects by administration specially on Pakyaw Contracting System, and the required supporting documents thereof; and 

(ii) the BAC to submit an explanation on the inconsistencies noted in the bidding documents for the purchase of garbage truck.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management (a) require the Municipal Accountant to record in the books the cost of the completed Multi-Purpose Building although there was no final payment yet and acceptance of the project was not effected by the Management. The fact that the LGU uses the building is tantamount to its acceptance; (b) create a special committee to account for its real properties specially the lots covered only by tax declarations for titling and proper recording in the books; (c) in reiteration to previous year’s recommendaton, create a special committee that shall undertake inventory of all fixed and non-fixed assets of the  Municipality including those funded by SEF to facilitate the preparation/update of required PPE records and reports; and (d) require the GSO and MAO to organize property cards and equipment/real property cards as a medium in the reconciliation of records.

We recommended that Management require (a) the MAO to (i) effect the recording of the liquidation of the DPWH funds in the books amounting to ₱2,385,794.08; and (ii) reclassify the funds erroneously identified as trust liability instead of income; (b) the MAO and the Municipal Treasurer’s Office to return the unutilized PDAF to the National Treasury totaling ₱5,347,005.92, and the unexpended balance of trust funds to the source agencies totaling ₱51,302.66; and (c) instruct the MAO to  exert efforts in retrieving records pertaining to ₱585,965.37 funds in order to facilitate the return of unused funds to source agencies.

We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accounting Office to (a) comply with the prescribed accounting entries in the transfer and utilization of the LDRRMF in accordance with PPSAS; (b) record the unexpended balances of the CY2018 to the TL- LDRRMF totaling ₱1,448,102.63; and (c) henceforth, strictly follow the relevant rules and regulations affecting the recording of the transfer and utilization of LDRRMF to reflect the correct balances of the related financial accounts.

We recommended that the MAO continue to exert its best possible means to retrieve further the previous records relative to the recording of the loans payables to reflect the true balance of the account.

We recommend that Management 

(a) require the concerned Municipal officials and employees to immediately settle their unliquidated cash advances and other obligations in accordance with the aforementioned rules and regulations; 



(b) make a representation with the LBP to require the approval of the concerned Municipal officials on Municipal employees seeking renewal of loan; and 

(c) henceforth, require the City Accountant to prepare ageing of cash advances on a regular basis for monitoring, and immediate preparation of demand letter to erring accountable officers to avoid the incurrence of the same deficiencies

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendation that the Local Chief Executive create a special committee that shall undertake the 

(i) inventory of fixed and non-fixed assets of the MGP; 



(ii) organize the property cards and equipment/real property cards of the GSO and Accounting Office, respectively; and

(ii) reconcile the discrepancies on non-fixed assets amounting to ₱16,224,933.19 between the Accounting and GSO records, and on the land account of ₱28,491,489.58.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to

(a) trace and review the details of the Due to NGA account balances with insufficient information, and 

(i) effect the adjustment in the books on fund transfers erroneously recorded to Due to NGAs, 

(ii) exert her best efforts in retrieving the financial records pertaining to the receipt and disbursements of the unexpended balance of trust funds totaling ₱563,855.37 in order to facilitate the return of funds to the National Treasury or source agencies, including the received funds in 2012 totaling ₱1,872,800.00 from Partylist and unknown sources, which were not utilized; 

(b) update the subsidiary ledger promptly to avoid overdrafts; 

(c) revisit the NGAS for proper use of accounts and recording of the flow of transactions; and 

(d) in coordination with the Local Finance Committee (LFC) to assess the income of the MGP that were treated as trust liability to augment its local resources and ensure that those funds are to be appropriated for the delivery of basic services to the constituents.

AAR CY-2017 | The MGP launched a project named "Malinis na paligid para sa maunlad na Bayan" that is aimed to inform the public on proper solid waste segregation, and promote composting and recycling. However still, various solid waste ordinances were not implemented.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the members of the LSB 

(a) refrain from charging expenditures from the SEF not authorized under the existing rules and regulation, including the claim of honoraria of DepEd officials and employees and training sports allowance to the members of the LSB and Municipal officials acting as advisors to avoid disallowance of payments, but rather develop high-impact and laudable program, projects, and activities consistent with the guidelines on the prioritization and utilization of SEF with complete regard to the Division’s annual improvement plan to ensure efficient and effective use of the Fund; 

(b) based on the DepEd classroom deployment analysis, intensify the 

(i) construction of new classrooms giving consideration to the growing population of the public school students on account of the escalating cost of tuition fees in the private schools; and 

(ii) repairs and maintenance of school buildings to attain an environment conducive to learning and prevent the disruption of classes during rainy season; and 



(c) comply with the rules and regulations prescribed under RA 9184 on public bidding to ensure that the government gets the most advantageous terms for the contract.
observations.managementAction:
Observation No.13
AAR CY-2017 | The use of funds to non-priority expenditures depleted the SEF funds for priority projects including the repairs and maintenance of school buildings.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the concerned Municipal officials to discontinue the payment of financial assistance to the MHO in view of his acting capacity as Municipal Administrator pursuant to the existing rules and regulations, and cause the refund of unauthorized payments pursuant to existing rules and regulations.
observations.managementAction:
Observation No.15
AAR CY-2017 |

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our previous year’s audit recommendation that the Local Chief Executive 


(a) create inventory committee to facilitate the completion of the inventory taking for all their properties and the preparation and submission of the corresponding report thereof, 

(b) require the Municipal Accountant and the GSO to maintain and update their respective property records and regularly reconcile them, 





(c) create disposal committee to take the appropriate action for the disposition of the unserviceable properties, and 

(d) require the Municipal Accountant to provide for depreciation for all depreciable properties.
observations.managementAction:
The LCE already created an inventory committee under Executive Order No. 22-16 dated December 20, 2016







The LCE already created a Disposal Committee under Executive Order No.22-16 dated December 20, 2016.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Engineer and General Services Officer to comply strictly with all the provisions of the aforementioned COA Circular for enhanced transparency on its operations and project implementation.
observations.reason: No copies of PPA’s were submitted to the Office of the Auditor. Also, the audit team was not informed about the postings of signboards or public notices in the location of the Municipality’s on-going projects.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local School Board revisit the laws, rules and regulations covering the use of the Special Education Fund and ensure compliance therewith.
observations.reason: The Municipality failed to align their disbursements with the revised guidelines on the utilization of SEF as enumerated in paragraph 4 of DepEd, DBM and DILG Joint Circular (JC) No. 1 series of 2017.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Government strictly adhere with the guidelines set by the DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-01 particularly on the utilization of the 20% Development Fund to ensure the attainment of desirable socio-economic development and environmental management outcomes.
observations.reason: The Municipality failed to fully follow the updated guidelines on the appropriation and utilization of IRA for development projects as prescribed under DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-001.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our previous year’s recommendation that Management 

(a) comply with the prescribed guidelines and procedures for the formulation, development, submission, implementation, monitoring and evaluation including accounting of results of agency annual GAD plans and budgets (GPDs), and GAD accomplishment reports (AR) as provided by PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No 2013-01; and 

(b) ensure the sufficient appropriation for GAD related activities/programs and its effective utilization for the intended purpose in order to comply fully with the provisions of the existing laws, rules and regulations with respect to GAD.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) require the (a) MTO to prepare the RCI with the date based on the actual issuance of check and submit to the MAO together with the paid DVs for preparation of the JEV; and (b) MAO to (i) prepare the JEV for the CkDJ based on the RCI submitted by the MTO to reflect the true cash in bank balances in the financial statements; (ii) reconcile records with the LBP particularly on the alleged bank error of ₱6,000.00; and (iii) henceforth, monitor closely the preparation of the BRS in order to effect the adjustments in the books, if any, in a timely fashion.

We recommend that Management (a) require the concerned Municipal officials and employees to immediately settle their unliquidated cash advances and other obligations in accordance with the aforementioned rules and regulations; (b) make a representation with the LBP to require the approval of the concerned Municipal officials on Municipal employees seeking renewal of loan; and (c) henceforth, require the City Accountant to prepare ageing of cash advances on a regular basis for monitoring, and immediate preparation of demand letter to erring accountable officers to avoid the incurrence of the same deficiencies.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the concerned accountable officer to (a) issue PCV for every payment made out of the PCF to ensure that only authorized petty expenses are paid out of the PCF; and (b) limit the payments from PCF to ₱15,000.00 and below pursuant to existing rules and regulations.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Local Chief Executive create a special committee that shall undertake the (i) inventory of fixed and non-fixed assets of the MGP; (ii) organize the property cards and equipment/real property cards of the GSO and Accounting Office, respectively; and (ii) reconcile the discrepancies on non-fixed assets amounting to ₱16,224,933.19 between the Accounting and GSO records, and on the land account of ₱28,491,489.58.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to (a) revert those payables still in the books for years, with no filed administrative or judicial claim, and not covered with perfected contract; and (ii) work on the abnormal balance in the records of the Other Payables account.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to retrieve the records relative to the recording of the loans payables to effect the adjustment in the books to reflect the true balance of the Loans Payable account.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to (a) trace and review the details of the Due to NGA account balances with insufficient information, and (i) effect the adjustment in the books on fund transfers erroneously recorded to Due to NGAs, (ii) exert her best efforts in retrieving the financial records pertaining to the receipt and disbursements of the unexpended balance of trust funds totaling ₱563,855.37 in order to facilitate the return of funds to the National Treasury or source agencies, including the received funds in 2012 totaling ₱1,872,800.00 from Partylist and unknown sources, which were not utilized; (b) update the subsidiary ledger promptly to avoid overdrafts; (c) revisit the NGAS for proper use of accounts and recording of the flow of transactions; and (d) in coordination with the Local Finance Committee (LFC) to assess the income of the MGP that were treated as trust liability to augment its local resources and ensure that those funds are to be appropriated for the delivery of basic services to the constituents.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) (a) ensure that (i) the infrastructure projects included in the LDIP for 2017-2019 that aim to direct the economic and social development of the Municipality be implemented efficiently and effectively by ensuring that the PPAs presented in the AIP were sourced from the LDIP of the Municipality, except on cases that require immediate action, provided further that the implementation of such passed the Local Development Council and resolved by the Sangguniang Bayan; (ii) the 20% DF is utilized in accordance with the existing guidelines; (iii) the procurements/projects are not split into smaller quantities and amount as provided for in R.A. No. 9184 to evade public bidding; and (b) require the (i) BAC, Engineering Office, and concerned officials to re-visit and review the process in implementing projects by administration specially on Pakyaw Contracting System, and the required supporting documents thereof; and (ii) the BAC to submit an explanation on the inconsistencies noted in the bidding documents for the purchase of garbage truck.

We recommended that the Management (a) initiate capacity building programs on GAD especially for the members of the GFPS to strengthen their knowledge and be able to perform their duties productively; (b) plan the establishment of a centralized GAD database to help in identifying emerging gender issues; (c) create a separate GAD office that will support and coordinate the initiatives of the GFPS and for proper monitoring and evaluation of all GAD related PPA's; and (d) ask the Sanggunian to formulate and pass a GAD Code or ordinance.

We recommended that the Management (a) revisit the provisions of the RIRR of RA No. 9184 specifically on the establishment, functions and duties of the BAC; and (b) cause the dissolution of the CoA and instruct the BAC to handle all procurements as required by existing law, rules and regulations.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the concerned Municipal officials to (a) reconstitute the SWMB to include the required members/representatives as prescribed in R.A. No. 9003; (b) expedite the necessary revisions and disposal of comments in the ESWMP for the review and approval of the Sangguniang Bayan and of the NSWMC; (c) strictly follow the rules and regulations set forth R.A. No. 9003, and monitor the implementation of various Ordinances made by the Sangguniang Pambayan in connection with solid waste management for strict enforcement; (d) invest more on facilities such as equipment in order to promote agriculture, and composting to decrease the cost of hauling, and lessen the residual wastes transferred to the sanitary landfill; (e) provide the segregators and collectors with personal protective gears to protect them from the waste hazards; and (f) induce the cooperation of business owners as well as the public commuters in maintaining the cleanliness of the surroundings.

We recommended that the members of the LSB (a) refrain from charging expenditures from the SEF not authorized under the existing rules and regulation, including the claim of honoraria of DepEd officials and employees and training sports allowance to the members of the LSB and Municipal officials acting as advisors to avoid disallowance of payments, but rather develop high-impact and laudable program, projects, and activities consistent with the guidelines on the prioritization and utilization of SEF with complete regard to the Division’s annual improvement plan to ensure efficient and effective use of the Fund; (b) based on the DepEd classroom deployment analysis, intensify the (i) construction of new classrooms giving consideration to the growing population of the public school students on account of the escalating cost of tuition fees in the private schools; and (ii) repairs and maintenance of school buildings to attain an environment conducive to learning and prevent the disruption of classes during rainy season; and (c) comply with the rules and regulations prescribed under RA 9184 on public bidding to ensure that the government gets the most advantageous terms for the contract.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive as the chairman of the LCPC, the MSWD Officer and other members of the LCPC comply with the requirements of the existing rules and regulations governing child protection.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the concerned Municipal officials to discontinue the payment of financial assistance to the MHO in view of his acting capacity as Municipal Administrator pursuant to the existing rules and regulations, and cause the refund of unauthorized payments pursuant to existing rules and regulations.

We recommended that Management require the Accounting Office to (a) ensure that the remittances of premiums and loan amortizations are intact; (b) make representations with the GSIS, Philhealth, and Pag-IBIG for reconciliation of records to address promptly the under(over) remittances of premiums and loans.

We recommended that Management require the concerned officers to (a) take concerted effort to determine the details of dormant balances, and promptly remit to BIR the amounts of recognized payables; and (b) strictly comply with the rules and regulations on remittances of withheld taxes as prescribed under Revenue Memorandum Order No. 23-2014 dated June 20, 2014 and Revenue Regulation No. 2-98 dated April 17, 1998; and c) ensure that withheld taxes are properly computed.
We recommended that Management observe the rules and regulations in the hiring of the J.O. personnel.

We recommended that Management enforce the settlement of disallowed amount of ₱484,500.00 affirmed by the COA Regional Office No. III, and no further action was made by the liable officials and employees within the prescribed period.

observations.managementAction: The share of Barangay San Jose amounting to ₱416,163.90 was already released. The concerned Municipal official committed to release immediately the 2016 RPT share of Barangay Pinalagdan amounting to Php 6,250.45.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management 

(a) track down the details and supporting documents of the unremitted taxes from prior years and accordingly remit them; and 

(b) to ensure that all taxes withheld are remitted in full as prescribed by the aforementioned regulation.
observations.managementAction: The Accountant of have already started but not have yet finished tracking down the unremitted taxes.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the concerned officials of the Municipal Government submit copies of perfected contracts, POs, JOs, notices of deliveries and inspection reports to the audit team within the prescribed period together with the supporting documents pursuant to the aforementioned regulations.
observations.reason: Copy of POs were submitted to the Office of the Auditor however, no Inspection and Acceptance Report/Notices of deliveries were submitted to the office.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendations that management 

(a) avail itself of the provision of Section 37 of PD 1445 to pave the way for the settlement of the former Mayor’s unsettled accountabilities; and

(b) instruct the Accounting Office to process the payment of claims due the estate of the late Mayor, in coordination with his family, and set off all unsettled accountabilities against said payable amount.
observations.managementAction: The family of the deceased accountable officer had not yet claimed his terminal leave benefits thus the recommenda-tions have not been implemented.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management instruct the Appraisal and Disposal Committee to   fast track the disposal of the unserviceable property to prevent further deterioration and generate additional income from the proceeds from sale thereof.

We recommended that Management 

(a) comply with the prescribed guidelines and procedures for the formulation, development, submission, implementation, monitoring and evaluation including accounting of results of agency annual GAD plans and budgets (GPDs), and GAD accomplishment reports (AR) as provided by PCW-NEDA-DBM Joint Circular No 2012-01; and 

(b) ensure the sufficient appropriation for GAD related activities/programs and its effective utilization for the intended purpose in order to comply fully with the provisions of the existing laws, rules and regulations with respect to GAD.
observations.reason: Reiterated under Observation No.8 of the CY2016 AAR

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Government; (a) release immediately the RPT shares due to the said barangays, otherwise, the filing of appropriate administrative and/or criminal action be instituted against the personnel responsible in the holding back of such shares and; (b) strictly comply with Section 271(d) of RA 7160 in releasing the RPT shares of the barangays.

We reiterated our previous year’s audit recommendation that the Local Chief Executive (a) create inventory committee to facilitate the completion of the inventory taking for all their properties and the preparation and submission of the corresponding report thereof, (b) require the Municipal Accountant and the GSO to maintain and update their respective property records and regularly reconcile them, (c) create disposal committee to take the appropriate action for the disposition of the unserviceable properties and (d) require the Municipal Accountant to provide for depreciation for all depreciable properties.

We recommended that Management (a) track down the details and supporting documents of the unremitted taxes from prior years and accordingly remit them; and (b) to ensure that all taxes withheld are remitted in full as prescribed by the aforementioned regulation.

We recommended that the concerned officials of the Municipal Government submit copies of perfected contracts, POs, JOs, notices of deliveries and inspection reports to the audit team within the prescribed period together with the supporting documents pursuant to the aforementioned regulations.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Engineer and General Services Officer to comply strictly with all the provisions of the aforementioned COA Circular for enhanced transparency on its operations and project implementation.
We recommended that the Local School Board revisit the laws, rules and regulations covering the use of the Special Education Fund and ensure compliance therewith.

We recommended that the Municipal Government strictly adhere with the guidelines set by the DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-01 particularly on the utilization of the 20% Development Fund to ensure the attainment of desirable socio-economic development and environmental management outcomes.

We reiterated our previous year’s recommendation that Management (a) comply with the prescribed guidelines and procedures for the formulation, development, submission, implementation, monitoring and evaluation including accounting of results of agency annual GAD plans and budgets (GPDs), and GAD accomplishment reports (AR) as provided by PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No 2013-01; and (b) ensure the sufficient appropriation for GAD related activities/programs and its effective utilization for the intended purpose in order to comply fully with the provisions of the existing laws, rules and regulations with respect to GAD.

We recommended that Management 

(a) release immediately the prior years’ RPT shares of the twelve barangays for their utilization on other programs and projects intended for their constituents; and 

(b) strictly comply with the provision on the full release of RPT shares in accordance with Section 271(d) of RA 7160.

We recommended that Management 

(a) instruct the GSO Office to prepare the property cards for control of the PPE and have it reconciled with the accounting records; 

(b) strengthen the controls on the handling and recording procedures for the PPE by performing a periodic reconciliation of the records of the accounting, the property and actual inventory results; and 

(c) obtain all records/documents of donated vehicles received by the agency for proper recording.

We recommended that Management 

(a)  assign other personnel from other offices who could be tapped to assist in the preparation of an accurate list of taxpayers and the amounts due there from as basis in the recording of the RPT/SET receivables; 
(b) require the Treasurer, in coordination with the Assessor’s Office, to prepare a certified list of taxpayers for submission to the Accountant at the beginning of the year;  and 

(c) require the Accountant to comply with the provision of Section 20, Volume l of the NGAS Manual on the setting up of the RPT/SET receivables based on the certified list of taxpayers.

We recommended that Management maximize and utilize fully the 20% development fund so that the desired outcomes for socio economic and development services to the various constituents will be fully attained.

We recommended that Management  require the 

(a) Local Development Council to formulate and incorporate LDRRMFIP plans and programs to ensure that utilization of LDRRMF would be in accordance with the intended purpose; and 

(b) comply strictly with Sections 4.4 and 5.1.2 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2002 on the allocation and implementation of LDRRMFIP.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive 
(a) request the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board to expedite the completion of the 10-year solid waste management plan; and 

(b) adopt measures to promote and ensure the viability and effective implementation of solid waste management programs of the respective component barangays.

We recommended that management continue to be compliant with Revenue Regulation No. 2-98 on the withholding of the appropriate taxes from its payment transactions and the remittance within the prescribed period.
